name: Louis Farrakhan
description: controversial cult leader of Nation of Islam
abstract: Louis Farrakhan (born Louis Eugene Walcott, later Louis X; May 11, 1933) is an American religious leader who has been the head of the Nation of Islam since 1981, an organization which combines black nationalism and Islamic teachings. Prior to the NOI, Farrakhan was a calypso singer who used the stage name Calypso Gene from 1950 to 1955 a…

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: It was Calypso Louis, who later became Louis Farrakhan. ... I figured, the hell with this. I’m going out. There’s a man sitting at the door, at a table, and he said, “You’re looking for somebody?” I said, “Yeah. Malcolm X.” ... Later we came to know each other.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Greg Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: with Mr. Louis Farrakhan and the Nation of Islam was pretty big at the time... we’d listen to tapes here
about_quotes.doc_title: Greg Thomas interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 2014
